title: Box Office 4-Day Weekend Forecast (Updated): AVATAR: FIRE AND ASH to Lead as MARTY SUPREME, ANACONDA, and SONG SUNG BLUE Bow on Crowded Christmas Frame
text: Avatar: Fire and Ash is projected to lead the Christmas weekend box office with a 3-day forecast of $61 million and a 4-day total of $80.4 million, bringing its projected domestic total through December 28 to approximately $206.4 million. The film is experiencing a 32% decline from its opening weekend, which the analysis notes was on the absolute low end of expectations. It will play across approximately 3,800 locations. Despite Avatar's softer-than-expected debut, industry analysts remain optimistic about its longevity. The forecast considers the franchise's history and anticipates a strong sophomore frame hold with continued premium screen demand. The analysis acknowledges that holiday box office projecting becomes particularly unpredictable during this period. Additionally, competition from streaming content is a consideration—specifically, Netflix's Stranger Things episodes releasing on Christmas night may impact theatrical attendance this weekend. Avatar: Fire and Ash maintains its position as the dominant theatrical release, significantly outpacing three newly debuting films this holiday corridor.
